ead.archdesc:
======= RMA00504Walter Francis Willcox papers14-10-504Compiled by N. DeanDivision of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Sept. 20032B Carl A. Kroch LibraryCornell UniversityIthaca, NY 14853rareref@cornell.eduURL: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-19 18:03:58 UTC.12/07/2018
This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)2007-05-29
converted from EAD 1.0 to EAD 2002 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Walter Francis Willcox papers
Willcox, Walter F. (Walter Francis), 1861-1964.
Petry, Loren C., 1887-1970.
14-10-504
7 cubic feet. 7 cubic feet.
16 microfilm reels. 16 microfilm reels.
4 audiocassettes. 4 audiocassettes.
1886-1960. Correspondence, tributes, articles, notes, genealogies. Papers concerning Willcox's career as professor of economics and statistics at Cornell, including extensive material on American social and political issues, as well as Willcox's personal and family papers. Collection material in English <<<<<<< HEAD 1 2 3 4 7022 14 - folder 1 14 - folder 1 15 - folder 1 15 - folder 2 16 - folder 1 16 - folder 2 ======= 1 2 3 4 7022 14 - folder 1 14 - folder 1 15 - folder 1 15 - folder 2 16 - folder 1 16 - folder 2 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab 2247173
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
N. Dean
Date completed:
October 1993
EAD encoding:
Ann Hubert, September 2003
Date Modified:
Marcie Farwell, December 2018
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Professor of economics and statistics at Cornell University.
RELATED MATERIALS
Includes collections #14-10-821, #14-10-mf32, #14-10-tr24, tr25, tr23, and tr22, and #14-10-m.76, m.376, and m.466.
Cite As:
Walter Francis Willcox papers, #14-10-504. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Scope and content
Correspondence, tributes, printed and draft articles and notes, and genealogies and other family papers. Subjects include Willcox's involvement in the United States census of 1900, the International Statistical Institute, Amherst College alumni activity, and statistical analysis of various social problems, including the registration of births and deaths and voter registration, Afro-American crime statistics, race relations, and Prohibition.
Scope and content
The collection also includes tape recordings celebrating Willcox's 90th, 95th, and 100th birthdays; family correspondence, including letters to and from Alice Eloise Work (Mrs. Walter Willcox), and his son Bertram F. who was in France with the American Ambulance Field Service.
Scope and content
Also, material relating to his work with the William Allen White Committee to Defend America by Aiding the Allies, comment on American political practices and statecraft, addresses and articles pertaining to Cornell University, and work concerning Congressional reapportionment. Tape recordings pertain to Andrew Dickson White, Jacob Gould Schurman, and Charles Kendall Adams.
Scope and content
Collection of anecdotes concerning various members of the Cornell University faculty and administrators, collected by Willcox for use by the Cornell Alumni News.
Scope and content
90th Birthday Dinner address, "The Disunited Nations: A Novel Experiment in the Balance of Power," and cover letter, April 2, 1951.
Scope and content
Memo by Loren Petry on the origin of the Willcox-Becker Luncheon Club (sometimes called the Thursday Luncheon Club), and comments by Walter Willcox on the club, plus his memories of his years (1899-1901) as chief statistician of the 12 U.S. Census. There is also an interesting reference to Edmund Ezra Day and freedom of speech.
Tributes. Economists. Professors emeriti. Race relations -- United States. Voter registration -- United States. Apportionment (Election law) -- United States. Families -- New York (State). African American criminals. Political science -- United States. Criminal statistics -- United States. Prohibition. Vital statistics. African Americans. Census -- 1900. Statistics. Work, Alice Eloise. Willcox, Bertram F. White, Andrew Dickson, 1832-1918. Schurman, Jacob Gould, 1854-1942. Adams, Charles Kendall, 1835-1902. Wilcox family. Work family. Cornell University -- : Faculty. International Statistical Institute Amherst College -- : Alumni. American Field Service William Allen White Committee to Defend America by Aiding the Allies Cornell University. Department of Economics
